extra.reasoning:
Property: NOT(rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b) for all inputs.

rec_drug_a: only rule rec_a_from_conjunction (priority 50) sets it: IF heart_failure THEN rec_drug_a := true. So rec_drug_a = heart_failure (else default false).

rec_drug_b: two rules, both with the identical condition heart_failure:
- guard_suppress_b (priority 90): rec_drug_b := false
- rec_b_from_trigger (priority 50): rec_drug_b := true
When heart_failure = true, both match; conflict resolution is per-output highest-priority-wins, and 90 > 50, so guard_suppress_b wins -> rec_drug_b := false. When heart_failure = false, neither rule matches and the default sets rec_drug_b := false. Therefore rec_drug_b is false for every input assignment.

Since rec_drug_b is unconditionally false, the conjunction (rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b) is false for every input, regardless of low_ejection_fraction or hepatic_impairment (which only affect rec_drug_c via priority-40 rules and are irrelevant here). The mutual-exclusion property holds across the entire 2^3 input space. The "true" rule for B is fully dominated by the higher-priority guard sharing the exact same trigger, so no input can ever make B true.
